RULING: The matter was argued. At the conclusion of arguments, the Court adopted its tentative ruling on PETITIONER THE ALLIANCE 1849 LLC.'s MOTION FOR ATTORNEY FEES AND EXPENSES, as follows:

Petitioner and Plaintiff The Alliance 1849 LLC's motion for attorneys' fees and costs is granted in the amount of $334,810.77 in attorneys' fees and $7,344.14 in costs.

Respondent and Defendant San Francisco Bay Conservation & Development Commission (BCDC) does not contest that petitioner, as the prevailing party in this action under the California Public Records Act (CPRA), is entitled under that Act to a mandatory award of court costs and reasonable attorney fees. (Gov. Code 6259(d); see Filarsky v. Superior Court (2002) 28 Cal.4th 419, 427; Belth v. Garamendi (1991) 232 Cal.App.3d 896, 899-900.) Petitioner seeks a total award based on a claimed lodestar of $363,993.45 and a multiplier of up to two (2.0), plus costs in the amount of $7,344.14. Petitioner also seeks an additional $30,000 in attorneys' fees incurred since it filed its motion.

[See word minutes for the Court's complete ruling.]

Order signed.

Judge: Ethan P. Schulman; Clerk: Gina Gonzales; Court Reporter: Maria A. Torreano CSR#8600, email: maria.torreano@gmail.com
